signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.42 — price holds above the 160.00 psychological level which previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. Despite intervention risk, the +292bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage sustains the bid while VIX remains at 16.4 (normal volatility). JPY COT LF net short -115,036 (long%=29.2%) indicates a crowded carry trade, while USD/JPY 25d RR at -1.06 signals the options market is paying a premium for downside protection against a potential BoJ/MoF response. Carry dominates until VIX > 25 or physical intervention occurs.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -22,354 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the July 8 meeting. Retail sentiment is 85% long, creating a sharp divergence where retail is fighting the institutional short. With NZD real rates at -0.83% (mildly accommodative) and CESI at -4 (negative bias), the pair faces structural headwinds. If RBNZ fails to deliver a hawkish surprise, retail capitulation risk targets 0.5800. NZD/USD at 0.5833.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hawkish Pause + Vol Premium
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7070 — RBA held rates at 4.35% but maintained a hawkish bias, signaling further hikes remain possible. AUD/USD IV at 21.8% vs HV30 at 8.1% creates a massive +13.7% Vol Risk Premium, indicating the options market is pricing in significant tail risk relative to recent realized movement. COT LF net long +38,092 (68.8%) shows specs are positioned for the hawkish outcome, but the -70bp SOFR-AONIA carry deficit vs USD limits upside momentum ahead of the FOMC.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Extreme
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3992 — correlation with WTI Oil has broken to -0.286 (z = -1.60σ), suggesting the pair is decoupling from energy prices to follow USD-centric yield drivers. Retail sentiment is 92% short, marking an extreme contrarian signal as retail fades the 1M +1.96% rally. Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% provides a +150bp carry advantage for USD, supporting the trend despite retail positioning. Watch for a break of 1.4000 to trigger retail stops.
